data.title: Aperam introduces Grade 316A as a cost-efficient alternative to 316L, offering performance and cost benefits.
data.description: Aperam has revealed Grade 316A, an innovative austenitic stainless steel that is now certified and patented. This new grade serves as a cost-effective option compared to Grade 316L.

data.title: Stock futures down on Friday. Advance Auto Parts expected to report 42 cents/share on $1.95 billion revenue. Roku beats estimates with 53 cents/share.
data.description: U.S. stock futures are down Friday morning, with investor focus likely on certain stocks. Advance Auto Parts Inc. (NYSE: AAP) is expected to announce quarterly earnings of 42 cents per share and revenue of $1.95 billion before the market opens. The company's shares climbed 1.3% to $59.00 in after-hours trading. Meanwhile, Roku Inc. (NASDAQ: ROKU) outperformed expectations in their fourth-quarter financial report, with quarterly earnings of 53 cents per share surpassing the estimated 27 cents by 93.43%. Additionally, they provided FY26 sales guidance that exceeded estimates. For more details, please refer to the complete story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Kalmar had a successful 2025 with record order intake and solid sales growth in Q4. Order intake was EUR 511 million, sales reached EUR 487 million, comparable operating profit improved to EUR 60.5 million. Operating cash flow increased by 76% to MEUR 112.8. Dividend proposal: EUR 1.09 per Class A share and EUR 1.10 per Class B share for 2025. Guidance for 2026: comparable operating profit margin expected to be above 12.5%. Kalmar achieved sales growth of 1%, a comparable operating profit margin of 12.8%, and ROCE of 23.0% in 2025. Financial targets for 2028: Sales growth of 5% p.a., comparable operating profit margin of 15%, ROCE above 25%. Kalmar's leverage ratio was 0.0 at the end of 2025, well below the long-term goal of a maximum of 2x. Megatrends driving the industry include safety, productivity, decarbonisation, and electrification. Strategic focus on sustainable innovations, services growth, and operational excellence. Kalmar's Board set performance targets for 2028 including sales growth of 5% p.a. and a comparable operating profit margin of 15%. Full article available on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Kalmar Corporation's financial review for January-December 2025 indicates record order intake of EUR 511 million, a considerable increase from the previous year's EUR 486 million. Sales also saw solid growth, reaching EUR 487 million, an 11% increase from the previous year. The operating profit for the year was EUR 220 million, representing 12.7% of sales. Noteworthy is the 76% increase in operating cash flow, reaching MEUR 112.8 million. The Board proposes a dividend increase for shareholders, with EUR 1.09 for class A shares and EUR 1.10 for class B shares. Looking ahead, Kalmar aims for a comparable operating profit margin above 12.5% in 2026. The report emphasizes the company's focus on sustainability, innovation, and operational excellence to maintain long-term value for shareholders. The company has a workforce of around 5,300 employees and operates in over 120 countries with a global installed base of 70,000 machines. Kalmar aims to address industry challenges such as safety, productivity, decarbonization, and intelligent operations through sustainable innovations, expanding services, and driving operational excellence. In 2025, the company achieved a sales growth of 1% and a comparable operating profit margin of 12.8%. Kalmar's performance targets for 2028 include sales growth of 5% annually, a comparable operating profit margin of 15%, and return on capital employed over 25%. The corporate vision emphasizes responsible business practices and a commitment to sustainable solutions for customers. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Kalmar gets order of 30 hybrid straddle carriers from Maher Terminals to support safe, sustainable operations.
data.description: Kalmar, a leading provider of cargo handling solutions, has recently announced a significant deal with Maher Terminals LLC. The agreement involves the purchase of 30 Kalmar hybrid straddle carriers by Maher Terminals LLC, a key client of Kalmar. This order highlights the strong relationship between the two companies and underscores Kalmar's expertise in providing efficient and sustainable solutions for container handling. The deployment of these hybrid straddle carriers is expected to enhance Maher Terminals LLC's operational capabilities and drive productivity.

data.title: Planet Labs PBC forms European Advisory Board & Defense and Intelligence Advisory Board at 2026 Munich Security Conference.
data.description: At the 2026 Munich Security Conference, Planet Labs PBC (NYSE: PL) revealed the establishment of two elite advisory bodies: the European Advisory Board and Defense and Intelligence (D&I) Advisory Board. These boards feature distinguished global figures with expertise in government, military, and diplomacy. The move signifies Planet's dedication to enhancing its services regarding daily insights on Earth's transformations. The announcement reflects the company's strategic emphasis on leveraging top-tier advice.
